summary: Doc-only audit triggered by 4 TestFlight/manual screenshots (2026-05-16, German locale) showing visible English copy in the Write pipeline nudge, Train consistency banner, Today header greeting, Today readiness check-in anchors, Today Focus suggestion reason, Today domain nudge, and Continue row subtitles. Traced each gap to its source: 6 files in Core/Domain and Core/Application build English copy directly (~30+ strings). The prior visible-string audit missed these because its 'ok-localized-display-helper' substring heuristic was too generous — names like nudge.message and draft.reason suggested upstream localization without confirming it. Wrote docs/workflows/localization-helper-generated-copy-audit.md and queued 7 narrow per-surface follow-up slices.
missing_proof_levels: running-app-smoke per locale (TestFlight is the canonical surface this audit traced)
screenshot-verified (we have the 4 input screenshots but no remediation screenshots)

contract_status_changes.before: Prior audits classified Text(nudge.message), Text(draft.reason), Text(headerGreeting) as ok-localized-display-helper based on variable-name heuristics. The upstream helpers in Core/Domain/CalibrationRules, Core/Domain/ReadinessRules, Core/Domain/PatternNudgeRules, Core/Domain/FocusSuggestionRules, Core/Application/TodayContinueSourceComposer, and Features/Today/TodayView.headerGreeting build English with let message = '...' directly. Result: TestFlight users in German see visible English in Today/Train/Write tabs.
contract_status_changes.after: Catalog documents ~30+ English copy fragments across 6 source files. 7 narrow follow-up slices queued, each surface-scoped. Approach hint per slice noted (in-place NSLocalizedString wrap vs structural refactor to move presentation copy out of Core/Domain back to Features/). Architecture concern flagged: 4 of 6 files violate the dynamic-formatting workflow boundary by owning UI copy in Domain.

residual_risks: Audit scope is limited to the 4 observed screenshots. Other view paths may contain additional helper-generated bypasses (e.g., Career view, Home view, notification body templates, widget surfaces). A deeper scan of Core/Application/* for return "..." with user-facing English may be warranted before claiming complete coverage.

Two valid fix approaches per slice (in-place NSLocalizedString wrap vs structural refactor). The audit recommends structural refactor for Domain-located code (preserves architecture) and in-place for Features-located code (no boundary concern). Slice owners may diverge.
Native review of any new keys remains outstanding. app-localization-native-review-intake stays blocked.
The audit method limitation (substring heuristic for 'ok-localized-display-helper') stays open. A future audit-method improvement could automate upstream-helper tracing.
The 7 follow-ups overlap in some Resources files (Localizable.strings); coordination needed if executed in parallel by multiple agents.

Architecture boundary in Core/Domain currently violated by 4 source files. Fixing the localization symptom in place (approach 1) preserves the violation. Structural refactor (approach 2) restores the boundary but is more invasive.
recommended_next_reason: ReadinessRules has the largest payload (9 messages) and is the most visible to users (header banner under the Today greeting). Fixing it first surfaces the structural-refactor pattern (return enum, format in Features/) that the other Core/Domain follow-ups can mirror.
